title: Elaeocarpus michaelii
taxonomy: Elaeocarpus michaelii is classified as a species within the Elaeocarpus genus and family Elaeocarpaceae. Elaeocarpus michaelii is a type of rainforest tree native to Australia, which aligns with the plant's classification as an Australian endemic. The Elaeocarpus genus is part of the larger Elaeocarpaceae family, which comprises around 250 species of flowering plants. Elaeocarpus michaelii's classification as a member of the Elaeocarpaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this group. As a member of the Elaeocarpus genus, Elaeocarpus michaelii shares characteristics with other species within this group.
uses: Elaeocarpus michaelii is a plant species native to Australia, and research has identified its potential medicinal uses. The plant's extracts have been found to have antimicrobial properties, which could be useful in treating infections.
